title: Does the cyclic group C_9135 admit a (9135,4567,2283)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would currently formalize only the surviving mod-7 and mod-29 quotient-profile lemmas. It would not seal a human-ready packet because the exact existence or nonexistence theorem for C_9135 remains unresolved after the failed w = 203 closure.
micro_paper_assessment: The exact 9135 survivor remains micro-paper-eligible in principle, but the audited packet is only a structural quotient-profile slice and is no longer close to a one-shot paper after the failed w = 203 closure.
family_affinity: strong cyclic Hadamard residual-row case; linked quotient-profile arithmetic and multiplier-orbit compression fit the family directly
generalization_signal: moderate-to-strong: combine two sharp small-prime quotient slices and block their combined-quotient lift under a common multiplier residue
strongest_honest_claim: Within the bounded audit using Baumert-Gordon Theorems 3.1 and 3.2 at w = 7 and w = 29, any cyclic (9135,4567,2283) difference set would have mod-7 contraction equal to one of (628,671,642), (649,671,635), or (682,660,635) up to swapping the two nonzero orbit values, and mod-29 orbit-constant profile (157,147,153,159,171) up to cyclic rotation. No exact existence or nonexistence theorem is currently established.
paper_title_hint: Forced mod-7 and mod-29 contraction profiles in the cyclic Hadamard case (9135,4567,2283)
candidate_theorem_slice: Using Baumert-Gordon Theorems 3.1 and 3.2 at w = 7 and w = 29, any cyclic (9135,4567,2283) difference set has mod-7 contraction equal to one of (628,671,642), (649,671,635), or (682,660,635) up to swap, and mod-29 orbit-constant profile (157,147,153,159,171) up to cyclic rotation.
publication_packet_quality: credible structural slice, not a paper-ready packet
next_action: Preserve the quotient-profile slice and move the slug aside unless a same-lane exact obstruction or direct construction for C_9135 is ready; do not expand into a broader theorem program.
